page: Google Cloud Platform c4d-highmem-96-lssd is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the c4d family. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs and 756.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of approximately 7.88 GB per vCPU. This configuration provides substantial memory capacity and compute density for workloads requiring significant memory footprints without the addition of discrete GPU hardware.

From a resource density perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent, non-shared performance across all 96 cores. The absence of integrated GPU accelerators makes this instance a cost-efficient choice for non-graphical, memory-heavy workloads, avoiding the premium associated with GPU-accelerated hardware. However, the lack of specialized hardware acceleration means it is not optimized for heavy machine learning training or real-time graphics rendering.

The high memory allocation and large core count make this server type suitable for memory-intensive enterprise applications. Appropriate workloads include large-scale databases, in-memory analytics, enterprise resource planning systems, and high-throughput data processing pipelines that require substantial RAM to prevent disk paging.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4d-highmem-96-lssd is an x86_64 architecture server instance designed for memory-intensive workloads. It is equipped with 96 dedicated vCPUs and 756.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 7.88 GB per vCPU. The instance features dedicated CPU allocation to ensure predictable performance under sustained compute loads. Lacking discrete GPUs, this server offers a cost-efficient profile for large-scale memory-bound tasks by avoiding the overhead of specialized accelerators. It is highly suited for enterprise databases, in-memory data processing, and large-scale analytical workloads that demand significant memory capacity alongside substantial parallel processing power.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4d-highmem-96-lssd is an x86_64 server featuring 96 dedicated vCPUs and 756 GB RAM, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads and large-scale databases.

tagline: A high-density x86_64 server configuration delivering ninety-six dedicated vCPUs and over seven hundred gigabytes of memory for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs for predictable parallel processing
756.0 GB of system memory providing 7.88 GB per core
No GPU accelerators, reducing costs for non-graphical workloads
